channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: “I’m sad to say it looks kind of like a graveyard” – Virtus.pro Fly on NA Dota 2
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to Virtus.pro’s Tal ‘Fly’ Aizik at ESL One Birmingham 2026 following his roster’s elimination in the group stage of the event.

The veteran Support spoke to Insider Gaming about the 7.41 patch, what Virtus.pro need to fix for future events, and the (lack of) hope for NA Dota 2.

Obviously, a group exit is not ideal. How are you feeling about the event?

It’s, of course, a bit sad to miss out. I think it was close as well because we were one win away from potentially going into tiebreakers.

So it makes it a little harder to take the loss. And we had like two really bad days. It was a bit rough.

0-2 losses to Aurora, Spirit, and Falcons seems fair enough – they’re very tough teams. Is it dropping games to OG and Nigma that comes to mind as points to regret?

I do think the three teams we lost to in the end were definitely like a level above us, for sure.

The teams we played previously, and we lost to, we were on closer footing. Looking back, if we had one more win against whoever, OG or Nigma, I think it was a bit more realistic.

But I think even thinking back on it, in the series against Falcons, I think we were in a decent position to at least win the first game. It’s just, at the end of the day, they executed better and won. But it was still a game, I think, we could have won.

Aurora games were not even close, to be completely honest. I think those were a complete shutout. But yeah, definitely a lot of junctures we could have gotten that extra win.

What did you think of the patch coming out?

I mean, first of all, I’m happy there’s a patch. I think it’s interesting for something new. And then I realised supports are getting a lot less XP on the map.

That was my first experience. I was like, ‘Huh, I’m usually level six by now, but I’m not.’ Then, the wisdom rune changes and stuff like that.

So I do think it’s overall an interesting change. The game feels more linear. Power spikes are not as huge compared to before.

So I like it. It’s going to take some time getting used to, and supports are going to need some extra XP somewhere. So it’s going to be interesting.

One of the heroes that you’ve played before is Tinker, and for some reason, Valve decided to kill him. Any thoughts on that?

I’m fine with Tinker being gone. He’s very toxic. You know what I mean? Like the hero, he kind of warps the games.

Some heroes just straight up change the way the game works, and he’s one of them. So I’m fine with it.

We’re not like, we’ve never been a huge Tinker team. I’m like, ‘Whatever.’ I like the hero, but he can go in the grave for a while.

Unfortunately, you didn’t make it to the stage. Have you had time to see what’s going on? Have you watched some of the games?

Yeah, I’m still watching the games. I’m still a Dota fan at the end of the day, and we have a competition coming up, so you want to see what the best teams are doing. So I’ve been watching games.

Yeah. So what do you think of the event?

The event is cool. I think it’s nice to see the fans turning up for this tournament again. So I’m excited to also get the feel of the crowd now that I’m here, and I’m going to watch some of the game.

It’s also interesting to see what teams come up with because you don’t have much time. I feel like a new patch needs at least a month, with a patch this big. So it’s a lot of chaos.

Now we see, Alchemist first picks, so it’s interesting to see what teams are going to bust out. And clearly Largo is still toxic, as Tundra showed us the other day. So we’ll see. We’ll see. It’s interesting.

You’re half Canadian. Do you think there is any hope for NA, or is it just despair?

It’s pretty despair, I would say. I feel like GamerLegion is really the only true North American team left that has some new talent with RCY. He’s very young.

But there are just no new players for Dota in NA, so I’m honestly sad to say it looks kind of like a graveyard.

All new players are mostly from Eastern Europe, so unless some organization was interested in picking up a roster and importing people, maybe building around like one, two, or three NA players, it’s pretty hard.

Yeah. Yeah. So, I mean, just in terms of like, you know, so you guys are going to go away from this tournament line and work.

What are you guys looking at to improve moving forward?

I think some of our fundamentals were lacking. For example, against Aurora, we got exposed in our laning phase very hard. So in the game, we never got the game to a stage where we could actually play.

We’re looking at all the small things we could do better in terms of laning. And then some of our map movement, our decision-making, and how we want to contest objectives, because I feel like every game was a bit of a different loss.

But the biggest difference between the best teams and us was noticeably the laning phase. So I think we’re going to start with that because I do think we have good ideas of how to actually play the game once we get out of the laning.

You can discuss the state of NA Dota 2 on our community Discord.

For more ESL One Birmingham interviews, see our full interview with Seleri.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fly-esl-one-birmingham-interview/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era Early Access Release Date Announced
item.content:encoded:
Nearly two years since it was first announced, Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era is heading into early access. On Wednesday, publisher Hooded Horse and developer Unfrozen revealed that the latest game in the 30-year franchise will launch into early access on April 30.

“It has been more than 10 years since the last Heroes game, and we are honoured to add a new chapter to the legendary legacy we all grew up with,” they said.

First announced in August 2024, Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era is a prequel to the events of the original 1995 game. The game will feature a full campaign, three multiplayer modes, challenges, and even a new arena mode for variety.

As far as what to expect from early access, Hooded Horse says the following will be available at launch:

  • Six unique factions: Temple, Dungeon, Schism, Grove, Necropolis, and Hive
  • An in-game tutorial
  • The first act of the campaign. More acts will be added with the full release of the game
  • Premade scenarios with self-contained stories, with even more coming during Early Access
  • Classic, Single Hero, and Arena modes, available in both single-player and multiplayer
  • Matchmaking, ranked play, and leaderboards for all three modes in multiplayer
  • A wide variety of Random Map Generator templates for unlimited replayability
  • Hotseat mode, allowing you to play against friends and family one after another on a single PC
  • A series of puzzle-style challenges
  • A pre-release version of map editor to create your own adventures

If you can’t wait for early access, there’s also a free demo available now via Steam to try the game out ahead of time. You can also check out the official early access trailer to get a glimpse of the new features in the game.

Are you going to pick up Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era when it launches into early access?  Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about the new studio formed by a former developer on Zenimax’s Project Blackbird. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.title: Every Video Game Releasing In April 2026
item.content:encoded:
Believe it or not, we’re already a quarter of the way through 2026 and that means April is here. As with any new month, there are dozens of video games releasing for everyone to enjoy. But sometimes it can be hard to keep up with every new release. Luckily, Insider Gaming has you covered with every video game releasing in April 2026.

Thanks to Insider Gaming community member Moru, who put together a fantastic spreadsheet of upcoming games, you can find all of April’s releases below. So give it a look, and let us know which games you’re most excited to play this month. And if you happen to see a game missing, leave it in the comments to be added to the list.

April 2026—Every Video Game Release

April 1, 2026

  • Goat Simulator 3 (Port) – Switch 2
  • Space Control (New Game) – VR (Steam VR, Quest)

April 2, 2026

  • Beat The Beats (New Game) – VR (Pico, Steam VR, Quest)
  • Clean Up Earth (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Switch 2, PC (Epic, Steam)
  • Cursed Words: The Word Game That Isn’t (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • Darwin’s Paradox (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Switch 2, PC (Steam)
  • Farmer’s Dynasty 2 (New Game) – PC (Epic, GOG, Steam)
  • Fishbowl (New Game) – PS5, PC (Steam)
  • Go! Go! Mister Chickums (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Xbox One, Switch, PC (Steam)
  • I Am Jesus Christ (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • Modulus (Full Release) – PC (Steam)
  • Morkull Ascend to the Gods (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Switch, PC (Steam)
  • Motion Rec (Port) – PS5, Switch
  • SPARK IGNITES (Early Access) – PC (Steam)
  • Temtem: Swarm (Full Release) – PS5, PC (Steam)
  • Xenonauts 2 (Full Release) – PC (Epic, GOG, Steam)

April 3, 2026

  • All Will Fall (Full Release) – PC (Steam)
  • Granser – Act 1 (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • Guns & Nuns: Storming Hell (New Game) – PC (Steam)

April 4, 2026

  • Kijutsu Rider (New Game) – PC (Steam)

April 7, 2026

  • KuloNiku: Bowl Up! (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • Narin: The Orange Room (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • Origament: A Paper Adventure (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • People Of Note (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Switch 2, PC (Epic, Microsoft, Steam)
  • PEPPERED: An Existential Platformer (Port) – PS5, PS4, Xbox Series X/S, Xbox One, Switch
  • Road to Vostok (Early Access) – PC (Steam)
  • Sea Of Stars (Port) – Android, iOS
  • Starfield (Port) – PS5
  • The Spotter: Dig Or Die (New Game) – PC (Steam)

April 8, 2026

  • Pokémon Champions (New Game) – Switch
  • Samson (New Game) – PC (Epic, Steam)

  • The House of Hikmah (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • The Occultist (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC (Epic, Steam)

April 9, 2026

  • Beyond Words (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Switch, PC (Steam)
  • Gears Of Glory (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • High Above (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • PowerWash Simulator 2 – Adventure Time Pack (DLC) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Switch 2, PC (Epic, Microsoft, Steam)
  • Restore Your Island (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • Solar Expanse (Early Access) – PC (Steam)
  • Sunken Sky (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • The Confinement (New Game) – PS5, PS4, Xbox Series X/S, Xbox One, Switch, PC (Steam)

April 10, 2026

  • Crystalfall (Early Access) – PC (Epic, Steam)
  • Fight Life: Vanguard (New Game) – PC (Steam)

April 13, 2026

  • Gunboat God (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC (Steam)
  • Oceaneers (Early Access) – PC (Steam)

April 14, 2026

  • A Storied Life: Tabitha (New Game) – Switch, PC (Steam)
  • Cascadou (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • City Transport Simulator 2026 (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC (Steam)
  • Dosa Divas (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Switch 2, Switch, PC (Steam)

April 16, 2026

  • Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC (Steam)

  • Gecko Gods (New Game) – PS5, Switch, PC (Steam)

April 17, 2026

  • RAD: Repeat After Death (New Game) – PC (Steam)
  • The Ember Guardian (New Game) – PC (Steam)

April 19, 2026

  • Saint Slayer: Spear Of Sacrilege (New Game) – PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Switch, PC (Steam)

April 20, 2026

  • Jay And Silent Bob: Chronic Blunt Punch (New Game) – PS5, PS4, Xbox Series X/S, Switch, PC (Steam, Xbox)

April 21, 2026

  • Foreign Sun (New Game) – Switch, PC (Steam)

Does Scritchy Scratchy Have Multiplayer Or Co-Op?

If you’re a fan of scratch cards, you need to give Scritchy Scratchy a try, but can you win big with friends?

Is Scritchy Scratchy On PlayStation, Xbox, Or Nintendo Switch? All Platforms, Explained

Scritchy Scratchy is a scratch card rogue-like that Steam users can’t get enough of, but is it available on consoles?

All Fortnite April Fools Update 2026 Content, Explained

The Fortnite 2026 April Fools’ Day update offers several huge new gameplay features that you need to know about right now.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Does Scritchy Scratchy Have Multiplayer Or Co-Op?
item.content:encoded:
There’s only one thing better than scoring a jackpot, and that’s doing it with friends! Considering that’s the name of the game with Scritchy Scratchy, there are bound to be some of you out there curious whether you can burn through those scratch cards with friends.

Once you get started in Scritchy Scratchy, you’ll unlock an Auto Scratcher, but is there a way to substitute out a robot for another friend? Sure, you might lose out on some wins, but there’s a chance you could beat them to a major jackpot, and the ensuing chaos would be hilarious.

Before you set off on your scratch card journey, keep on reading to see if it’s a solo endeavour, or if you can win big with some friends in Scritchy Scratchy.

Does Scritchy Scratchy Have Multiplayer Or Co-Op?

It’s sad news for those of you out there wanting to play Scritchy Scratchy with friends, as it’s strictly single player.

This sentiment extends to both online and local multiplayer, as there’s only room for one when it comes to chasing jackpots. It fits the trend of these titles, with similar games like Balatro and Raccoin also being limited to single player.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Scritchy Scratchy On PlayStation, Xbox, Or Nintendo Switch?

Steam also makes the choice clear, as it lists the game as ‘Single-Player,’ so the closest thing you’ll get to another helping hand is the Auto Scratcher. Considering a major upgrade path is speeding up the Auto Scratcher, it makes sense not to include multiplayer, as a major asset of the game might be made redundant.

Will Scritchy Scratchy Add Multiplayer Or Co-Op?

It remains unknown whether Scritchy Scratchy will ever add multiplayer or co-op, but based on the lack of information by the developers, it seems incredibly unlikely.

As things stand, the developers haven’t shown any signs that Scritchy Scratchy will be getting a multiplayer add-on, regardless of whether it’s online or local.

  • More Insider Gaming: Does Slay The Spire 2 Early Access Have Multiplayer?

However, that’s not to say that it couldn’t happen in the future. Development cycles are always shifting and adapting, so who knows what the future holds? But for right now, you’ll have to keep to yourself and rely on the numerous in-game upgrades to rake in those jackpots.

Would you play Scritchy Scratchy multiplayer? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming multiplayer guides, check out the following:

  • Retro Rewind Video Store Simulator
  • Pokémon Pokopia

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Saros Has Gone Gold a Month Ahead of Release
item.content:encoded:
Housemarque has announced that Saros has Gone Gold today, a month ahead of its April 30 release date.

Earlier this month, it was revealed that Housemarque had multiple job openings for their next project, just a few days after Saros was featured during February 2026’s State of Play event. Now, they have made another announcement about their upcoming title, which many players will be happy about.

Saros Has Gone Gold on April 1, 2026

The announcement that Saros has Gone Gold was made through an official post from the Housemarque account on X (formerly Twitter). The developers emphasized that this was not an April Fool’s joke. For those who may not know, when a developer states that their game has “gone gold,” it means the title has reached a stage where it can be sent to manufacturers for physical copies to be produced, indicating that no further delays are expected.

What is Saros About?

For those who are unaware, Saros is an upcoming PlayStation 5 exclusive developed by Housemarque, the team behind Returnal. In this new game, players will take on the role of Arjun Devraj, a Soltari enforcer on a quest for answers in the shape-shifting world of Carcosa. Saros features a Second Chance mechanic, a constantly evolving environment, and a high-tech arsenal of weaponry which players can use to “dodge, shield, and parry enemy attacks in a fluid, flow-state-inducing dance.”

What are your thoughts on Saros going Gold a month ahead of release? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Shift Up acquiring Unbound and publishing its upcoming titles. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
